source: Ash feature request 2026-06-21 â€” route each turn through the right profile without cross-bleed or races.
title: Profiles: turn-scoped profile switching + concurrency safety
current_state: agent/conversation.py::conversation_switch already saves/restores conversation_id + messages around a turn, and the brain server serializes turns with a FIFO asyncio.Lock. There is no equivalent for profile: the agent has one memory, one consciousness, one namespace for all turns.
target_state: A profile_switch(agent, profile) context manager (mirroring conversation_switch) sets agent.current_profile, points memory to the per-profile MemoryTools (T-221), selects the per-profile consciousness prompt, and RESTORES all of it in a finally block. Telegram turns wrap process_input in profile_switch(resolved_profile) AND conversation_switch (per-profile conversation_id, e.g. telegram:<chat_id> stored in the profile's own DB). Switching is turn-scoped and runs inside the existing serialization lock, so concurrent Ash+guest turns can't interleave state. Prompt assembly selects the profile's prompt each turn (no cross-profile cache bleed).
migration_plan: 1. agent/profile.py: profile_switch(agent, profile) contextmanager â€” save agent.current_profile + agent.memory ref + active consciousness; set them for the profile; restore in finally (exact shape of conversation_switch).

2. tools_telegram._process_as_telegram_peer: resolve profile from chat_id (T-222), then `with profile_switch(agent, profile): with conversation_switch(agent, conv_id): process_input(...)`. conv_id resolves within the profile's own DB so guest conversations never touch pi.db.

3. agent/prompt.py build_system_prompt_split: take the consciousness/prompt from the active profile (ash -> consciousness.txt; guest -> guest prompt from T-224). Ensure the per-profile static segment is what gets cached (no shared static across profiles).
4. Confirm ordering: profile_switch is OUTERMOST so memory + prompt are correct before conversation context loads.

5. Tests: testing/test_profile_switching.py â€” a guest turn then an ash turn: ash's messages/memory unchanged; guest turn reads/writes only the guest DB; current_profile restored after each turn even on exception; nested switch/restore leaves agent state identical to start.
risk_notes: Order matters (profile outer, conversation inner). Must restore on exception. Rely on the existing FIFO lock for serialization â€” do NOT add a second locking scheme. Keep the context managers symmetric and boring.
